## [06:04 - 09:06]

important thing I had to do is, in the spirit of his family, is very often in life, when we go through stress, often what happens is people roll reverse. You know, adults very often today will roll reverse with their kids, and they have their kids coaching them or counseling them or taking care of them or looking stupid compared to their kids, and that may feel good for the moment, because you feel like you got somebody there for you, but long term, it's damaging to yourself, it's damaging to your kids, and so how to make that roll reversal happen? So our strategy was simple, get them out of the environment they're in, give them a home run, put them in this incredible environment like they've never seen, put them in this beautiful villa on the ocean with a giant swimming pool, just to jolt them, and some people say, well, you can never do that at home, we can never get on a plane and go to Fiji, or we can never go, you know, fly a plane, or I hear people all the time talking about what they can't do, and it tricks me, makes me crazy. Of course you can, of course you could find a way to get together six or seven or eight hundred dollars and get a ticket to Fiji, of course you could find a way to achieve whatever you want. That belief is what's keeping you from having a breakthrough, and so we weren't doing this to just like, you know, give somebody a gift, we did this because there's a principle in human behavior and conditioning. If you want to train somebody, we're always training each other how to be, how to think, how to feel by what we get rewarded for. If every time you get sad and angry, somebody comes and pays a attention to you and gives you love, then you're always going to get sad or angry, have a problem. If every time you step up, people acknowledge you, and you really feel that acknowledgement, you're going to get rewarded, but we often reinforce people for the wrong behaviors, and we wonder why they behave the way they do. In animals, with a dog, some dog trainers use pain, they can get pain to an animal if they don't do what they want, but human beings aren't like dogs. Human beings are more like dolphins. If you want to train a dolphin, and you try to beat it, that won't work. They swim away. The only way you can train a dolphin is you wait for a dolphin, a dolphin that's maybe sick or it's not, it's lazy and it hasn't done any jumping, and you want to train it to jump. What they'll often do is create what's called a jackpot. A jackpot is they come in and they drop an entire bucket of fish on this little dolphin who's been doing nothing, quote unquote, doesn't deserve it, but what happens is they get so stimulated by all this fish being around that there's a new energy in them, and they start to jump and then they start to reinforce them. So my view was let's take these kids, they didn't create this environment, let's take John and let's give him a jackpot. Let's put him in Fiji, let's give him this experience, let's show him what's really possible, and anyone can do this also, but we're going to do it for him, but then he's going to have to do the hard work, and we're going to have to change the kind of reverse the roles. So how we did that was with the bridge jump. And by the way, in case you didn't notice, the bridge jump is something I've done for years. It's Plice and Fiji. It's this river that comes from the ocean when it reverses direction, the ocean comes to high tide. It comes splashing through this river at this incredible velocity, and I went and jumped in at one time in the daytime and I couldn't swim back. Then I decided wouldn't it be scary at night and I did it and I've been going there for 20 years. So I thought this would be a great task because John's
